name: worktree-gc
description: SessionStart hook that prunes merged-but-undeleted LINKED git worktrees in the cwd repo, implemented in hexa-lang (`_worktree_gc.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Threshold gate (linked-count >= 3 OR disk avail < 20 GiB) means the common case (1-2 worktrees, ample disk) stays silent. Above threshold, fetches `gh pr list --state merged --limit 200` and considers a worktree only when its branch is in that merged set OR its HEAD commit truly landed on origin/main. 0.2.0 adds FOUR unconditional live-work guards so an active worktree is NEVER wiped mid-task: (a) dirty-tree (uncommitted changes → SKIP), (b) recent-mtime (HEAD commit or any file modified < 1h → SKIP), (c) cwd-in-use (a live process' cwd is in the worktree, via lsof → SKIP), (d) HEAD-ancestor real-merged check (`git merge-base --is-ancestor <wt-HEAD> origin/main`) — a vanished `origin/<branch>` ref alone is IGNORED so a reused branch name can't condemn fresh work. (e) Atomic prune: `git worktree remove` is the sole removal path; on failure NOTHING is deleted (no partial / manual rm). Open-PR worktrees are NEVER touched. gh unavailable / no merged PRs → short note + no action. Always exits 0 (non-blocking). NO opt-out by design.
